text.title: Freelance Referral Program Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Freelance Referral Program Design. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Incentive MUST be a flat $ amount or credit. Percentage-based referral fees are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Percentage incentives create conflict-of-interest pricing distortion; flat amounts keep transparent expectations.

rule.statement: Each referrer MUST have written incentive terms ON FILE before the first referral closes.
rule.rationale: Verbal terms invite disputes when payouts grow.

rule.statement: Testimonial framing for referrals MUST be pre-approved with the customer: logo + outcome description; no client-confidential numbers without explicit clause.
rule.rationale: NDA leaks via casual testimonials damage client trust + may breach contract.

rule.statement: Referral attribution window MUST be ≤90 days from the introduction. Leads closing after that window are organic.
rule.rationale: Open-ended attribution makes accounting impossible and creates double-paying risk.

rule.statement: Partner-swap pairs (mutual referral) MUST be explicitly opted in by both sides in writing. Implicit reciprocity is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit swap dynamics breed resentment when reciprocity feels uneven.
